ideas.intro: Ideas for projects, activities, books, games, and more for students with a high level of interest.

items.content:
[ ] (Bible) Act out a memorable Bible reading from the term.
[ ] (Old Testament) Use scraps of fabric to sew a simple "coat (or cape) of many colors" for a stuffed animal or doll. (Tie dye is another option.)
[ ] (Old Testament) [Explore the size of Noah's Ark. ](https://arkencounter.com/noahs-ark/size/)
[ ] (Old Testament) Use scraps of fabric to sew a simple "coat (or cape) of many colors" for a stuffed animal or doll. (Tie dye is another option.)

[ ] (Bible) Create a Bible shoebox diorama using recycled objects, outdoor objects, and/or play figures to go with one of the readings from this term. You can find many ideas on Pinterest for inspiration.
[ ] (Bible) Explore the [virtual resources](https://www.museumofthebible.org/museum-from-home) at the Museum of the Bible. (Note: a parent should preview.)
[ ] (New Testament) Share a meal with others such as taking donations to a food bank, taking a meal to a neighbor, or baking cookies for a local fire or police station.
[ ] (Old Testament) Listen to[ Psalms set to music](https://thepsalmsproject.com/music), or create your own music (you could use piano, rhythm sticks, tambourine, or drum) to accompany one of the Psalms you read this term.

[ ] (New Testament) Spend time fishing as a family and try to name all the times fishing or fish are mentioned in this term's readings.
[ ] (New Testament) [Create a "Resurrection Garden." (These can also be used as centerpieces for Easter dinner.)](https://seedskidsworship.com/resurrection-garden/)
[ ] (Bible) Share a meal with others such as taking donations to a food bank, taking a meal to a neighbor, or baking cookies for a local fire or police station.
